data.title: Pretext setup + base text components with existing font styles

data.body: Install Pretext library. Create base text rendering components (Heading, Body, Quote, Label) configured with existing font stack: Space Grotesk (body), Space Mono (labels/nav), Instrument Serif (logo). All text on the site will render through these Pretext primitives — CSS handles structural layout only (grid, flexbox, box model). Reference: research/01-pretext-capabilities.md

## Review: FRAC-23 Pretext Setup + Base Text Components **Reviewer:** agent:claude-opus-4-reviewer **Verdict:** PASS (with minor notes for future improvement) ### Acceptance Criteria Checklist 1. **@chenglou/pretext installed and importable** -- PASS. Version ^0.0.3 in artifacts/fractal-nyc/package.json, resolves correctly. 2. **Google Fonts loads Space Grotesk, Space Mono, Instrument Serif, Jacquard 24** -- PASS. The @import URL in index.css loads all four: Space Grotesk (400-700), Space Mono (400,700), Instrument Serif (regular+italic), Jacquard 24. 3. **Tailwind --font-sans = Space Grotesk, --font-mono = Space Mono** -- PASS. Set in @theme inline block in index.css. Also adds --font-serif = Instrument Serif (not required but sensible). 4. **Three Pretext components exist** -- PASS. PretextHeading, PretextParagraph, PretextLabel all created with barrel export. 5. **Components use prepareWithSegments + layoutWithLines** -- PASS. The usePretext hook correctly uses both functions, with proper memoization (prepared memoized on text/font/fontsReady, layout re-run on width/lineHeight change). 6. **Components await document.fonts.ready** -- PASS. useFontsReady hook waits for the promise before Pretext measurement begins. 7. **Components re-layout on resize via ResizeObserver** -- PASS. usePretext hook attaches ResizeObserver to container, updates width state, which triggers layout recalculation. 8. **Fallback to regular DOM text when Pretext unavailable** -- PASS. All three components render native DOM elements (h1-h6, p, span) with matching styles when ready is false or lines is null. Try/catch wraps Pretext calls. 9. **Existing site not broken** -- PASS. No modifications to any files under root src/ or root package.json. The existing site's index.css and components are untouched. 10. **TypeScript typecheck passes** -- PASS. `pnpm --filter @workspace/fractal-nyc typecheck` completes with zero errors. ### Code Quality Notes **Architecture is solid.** The usePretext hook pattern is well-structured: font readiness gating, memoized preparation, cheap re-layout on resize. Error boundaries via try/catch provide graceful degradation. The separation into useFontsReady / usePretext / lib/pretext.ts is clean and follows the plan exactly. **PretextParagraph p-to-div transition:** Fallback renders

, ready state renders

. This is correct because

cannot contain display:block children. The role='paragraph' preserves accessibility semantics. Minor note: the element swap during hydration could cause a brief flash, but since this is CSR (Vite SPA), not SSR, this is a non-issue. ### Minor Notes (Not Blocking) 1. **PretextLabel measurement mismatch:** The component applies textTransform:'uppercase' and letterSpacing:'0.1em' via CSS, but Pretext measures the original lowercase text without letter-spacing (canvas measureText doesn't apply CSS transforms). For short label text this is unlikely to cause visible issues, but line breaks could be slightly off for longer labels. A future improvement would be to pass children.toUpperCase() to usePretext. Not blocking because labels are typically single-line. 2. **autoInstallPeers lockfile change:** The pnpm-lock.yaml changed autoInstallPeers from true to false. This matches the .npmrc config (auto-install-peers=false), so the lockfile was previously inconsistent. The re-resolution corrected it. Not a regression. 3. **Ref type casting:** Components cast containerRef (HTMLDivElement) to HTMLHeadingElement/HTMLSpanElement/HTMLParagraphElement. Functionally safe since ResizeObserver works on any Element, but could be made type-safe with a generic usePretext. Low priority. 4. **lib/pretext.ts wrapper functions:** preparePretextSegments and layoutPretextLines are thin wrappers that add no logic over the raw Pretext imports. The hook imports Pretext directly rather than using these wrappers. The wrappers are still useful as a centralized import point for future consumers. No action needed.
